message:
chore(matrix): export 8 libcrypt aliases via libc.map + support_matrix (4010 sym, bd-xapiq)

Roll the ABI bookkeeping forward to absorb the eight new symbols
shipped by the bd-xapiq libcrypt batch:

crates/frankenlibc-abi/version_scripts/libc.map
  - GLIBC_2.2.5 export block "/* crypt */" group grows by eight
    lines, alphabetized:
      crypt_checksalt
      crypt_preferred_method
      encrypt
      encrypt_r
      fcrypt
      setkey
      setkey_r
      xcrypt
    The existing `crypt;` entry is unchanged.

support_matrix.json
  - total_exported: 4002 -> 4010 (matches the eight new entries).
  - The existing `crypt` entry's strict/hardened semantics strings
    rewritten from the historical "Passthrough to glibc crypt"
    placeholder to the actual implementation contract:
      "Native Rust crypt implementation for $1$ MD5, $5$ SHA-256,
       and $6$ SHA-512 salts; invalid inputs return NULL with EINVAL"
      (hardened: "Same native implementation with pointer null
       guards and deterministic invalid-salt rejection")
    so the matrix no longer mis-claims that we route through
    glibc when in fact we do not.
  - Eight new entries, all status=Implemented + module=unistd_abi:
      crypt_checksalt:        coldpath; libxcrypt-compatible salt
                              prefix classifier ($1$/$5$/$6$ ->
                              CRYPT_SALT_OK; everything else ->
                              CRYPT_SALT_INVALID; NULL -> invalid).
      crypt_preferred_method: coldpath; returns the static "$6$"
                              SHA-512 prefix.
      fcrypt, xcrypt:         coldpath; historical libcrypt aliases
                              that delegate to the native crypt
                              implementation.
      encrypt, encrypt_r,
      setkey, setkey_r:       coldpath; deterministic DES no-ops —
                              DES is unsupported by native crypt
                              and these names exist purely so
                              programs that link against them load
                              instead of erroring at startup.

tests/conformance/support_matrix_maintenance_report.v1.json
  - Generator picked up the eight new entries + the rewritten
    crypt strict/hardened strings; per-status counts and
    per-module deltas refreshed accordingly. The 52-line diff is
    the standard "regenerate against the new matrix" rewrite.

Pure bookkeeping; no library code touched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
   "version": 2,
   "generated_at_utc": "2026-02-18T04:49:26Z",
   "library": "/data/tmp/cargo-target/release/libfrankenlibc_abi.so",
-  "total_exported": 4002,
+  "total_exported": 4010,
   "taxonomy": {
     "Implemented": "Native Rust code owns the entire operation. No host libc dependency. Full test coverage required.",
     "RawSyscall": "ABI entrypoint marshals arguments directly to Linux syscall instruction. No glibc call-through.",
@@ -2458,8 +2458,80 @@
       "status": "Implemented",
       "module": "unistd_abi",
       "perf_class": "default",
-      "strict_semantics": "Passthrough to glibc crypt",
-      "hardened_semantics": "Passthrough to glibc crypt",
+      "strict_semantics": "Native Rust crypt implementation for $1$ MD5, $5$ SHA-256, and $6$ SHA-512 salts; invalid inputs return NULL with EINVAL",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same native implementation with pointer null guards and deterministic invalid-salt rejection",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "crypt_checksalt",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "libxcrypt-compatible salt prefix classifier: returns CRYPT_SALT_OK for $1$/$5$/$6$ and CRYPT_SALT_INVALID for NULL or unsupported prefixes",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same classifier with null pointer guard",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "crypt_preferred_method",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Returns static preferred crypt method prefix $6$ for SHA-512",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same static process-lifetime string",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "encrypt",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Obsolete DES helper compatibility entrypoint; deterministic no-op because DES is unsupported by native crypt",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same deterministic no-op",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "encrypt_r",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Reentrant obsolete DES helper compatibility entrypoint; deterministic no-op because DES is unsupported by native crypt",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same deterministic no-op",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "fcrypt",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Historical libcrypt alias of crypt using the native Rust crypt implementation",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same alias behavior as crypt",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "setkey",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Obsolete DES key schedule compatibility entrypoint; deterministic no-op because DES is unsupported by native crypt",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same deterministic no-op",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "setkey_r",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Reentrant obsolete DES key schedule compatibility entrypoint; deterministic no-op because DES is unsupported by native crypt",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same deterministic no-op",
+      "default_stub": false
+    },
+    {
+      "symbol": "xcrypt",
+      "status": "Implemented",
+      "module": "unistd_abi",
+      "perf_class": "coldpath",
+      "strict_semantics": "Historical libcrypt alias of crypt using the native Rust crypt implementation",
+      "hardened_semantics": "Same alias behavior as crypt",
       "default_stub": false
     },
     {
